title: Fix GAE deploy: add .gcloudignore and set max_instances
Write tests for albums
description: Unit tests for Album model and data layer. E2E: album list page renders, link-out URLs are correct.

title: Design Album model and admin
description: Create an albums app (or add to members app). Album model: title, event_year, external_url (Google Photos, iCloud, etc.), thumbnail_url (optional), description, created_at. Django admin: list, add, edit. Service layer.

title: Build member-facing album list page
description: Members-only page listing all albums by event year. Each album card: title, year, thumbnail (if set), link-out button to external album. No upload functionality — links only.

title: Write tests for financial models and views
description: Unit tests for PaymentRecord and KittyExpense models, data layer, and logic layer (totals, balance calculations). E2E: member sees their payment status, kitty summary page loads with correct totals.

title: Build kitty summary view (members only)
description: Members-only view showing: total kitty collected, total kitty expenses, remaining balance, expense breakdown by category. Read-only. Gives members transparency on shared finances.

title: Build admin interface for payment tracking
description: Full ModelAdmin for PaymentRecord: list display (user, amount, status, date), filters by status, bulk approve action. ModelAdmin for KittyExpense: list, filters by category. Inline summary totals in changelist header or sidebar.

title: Build member payment status view
description: Members-only view showing: their own payment history, total paid, outstanding balance (based on expected contribution defined by admin). Read-only. Mobile-friendly layout.

title: Design payment tracking models (PaymentRecord, Kitty)
description: Create a finances app. Models: PaymentRecord (user FK, amount, currency, date, notes, status: pending/received), KittyExpense (description, amount, paid_by, date, category). Migrations. Services layer: data.py for CRUD, logic.py for totals/summaries.

title: Build full event details page (members only)
description: Full event information page: dates, location, accommodation details, schedule overview, costs breakdown, what to bring, FAQs. Content managed via Wagtail StreamField so it can be updated without deploys.

title: Build event itinerary page (members only)
description: Day-by-day schedule for the event. Wagtail StructBlock-based itinerary: day, time slots, activity, notes. Readable on mobile. May include maps links.

title: Build member dashboard / home page
description: Authenticated landing page for members. Shows: welcome message, upcoming event summary card, quick links to itinerary, payment status, member list, and albums. Wagtail page or Django view (decide based on ADR).

title: Implement members-only access control
description: Add login_required decorator / LoginRequiredMiddleware to enforce authentication on all non-public URLs. Unauthenticated users redirect to login page. Approved-but-not-yet-profiled users redirect to profile setup. Configure LOGIN_URL, LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL settings.

title: Build member list / who's coming page
description: Members-only list of confirmed attendees. Shows: display name, profile photo (if set), any other public profile fields. Sourced from approved User/Profile records. No editing from this view.

title: Write tests and e2e tests for member portal
description: Unit tests for member access control. E2E: unauthenticated redirect, authenticated member can access dashboard/details/itinerary/member list, non-approved user sees appropriate error.

title: Configure Wagtail admin access and editor permissions
description: Set up Wagtail admin access: superusers can do everything; designate an 'editor' group with permission to edit page content but not manage users. Document how to promote a member to editor.

title: Write tests and e2e tests for public pages
description: Unit tests for page models. E2E Playwright tests: homepage loads without login, EventTeaser loads, login redirect works on protected pages. Verify public pages return 200 for unauthenticated requests.

title: Build public HomePage (landing page)
description: Create HomePage Wagtail page model. Public, no login required. Content: event name hero, brief description of the group/event, teaser for next event, call-to-action to request access or log in. StreamField for flexible content editing by non-technical admins.

title: Build public EventTeaser page
description: A public-facing page giving non-members a taste of the event: what is it, who's behind it, when/where (if appropriate to reveal publicly), and a prompt to request access for full details. Editable via Wagtail admin.

title: Configure Tailwind CSS v4 and base templates
description: Set up Tailwind CSS v4 (same pinned approach as menus). Create base template with navigation: public header (login/request access), authenticated header (member nav). Mobile-first, dark mode support. Define colour palette and typography for the WSDE brand.

title: Design Wagtail page type hierarchy
description: Plan and document the Wagtail page tree: RootPage → HomePage (public), EventPage (public teaser), MembersSection (gated), etc. Decide which pages are Wagtail StreamField-based vs Django views. Capture in a design doc or ADR.

title: Install and configure Wagtail CMS
description: Add Wagtail to the project. Configure INSTALLED_APPS, Wagtail settings (WAGTAIL_SITE_NAME, WAGTAILIMAGES_*, etc.), Wagtail URLs (wagtail_serve, wagtail admin, API if needed). Ensure Wagtail admin is accessible and migrations pass.

title: Design and implement UserRequest model
description: Create a users app. Add UserRequest model: email, name, message, status (pending/approved/rejected), created_at, reviewed_at, reviewed_by. Migrations. Django admin with approve/reject actions. Service layer: data.py and logic.py.

title: Write tests for authentication flows
description: Unit tests (pytest-django) for: UserRequest model and logic, approval/rejection logic, login gate adapter. E2E Playwright tests: request access form submission, Google login (mocked or real staging test account), blocked login redirect.

title: Implement login gate: block non-approved social logins
description: Hook into django-allauth's socialaccount_adapter to block sign-up for emails not in the approved list. Non-approved users should see a friendly 'you don't have access yet' page with a link to /request-access/. Existing approved users can always log in.

title: Create Member profile model
description: Add a Profile model (OneToOne with User): display name, phone number, dietary requirements (for event planning), joined_at. Admin registration. Service layer. Shows up in member directory.

title: Build request-access form and email notification
description: Public form at /request-access/ where non-members can request an account. On submit: create UserRequest, send notification email to admin(s). Use Django's email backend. Template for the form and success page.

title: Implement admin approval flow for user accounts
description: When admin approves a UserRequest: create or activate the Django user account, optionally send welcome email with login link. When rejected: send rejection email. Add AllowedEmail model so only approved emails can complete social login.

title: Configure Apple Sign-In provider (post-spike)
description: Implement Apple Sign-In via django-allauth after spike confirms feasibility. Requires: Apple Developer account app registration, Services ID, private key (.p8), and domain verification. Configure allauth Apple provider. Test on staging with a real Apple ID.

title: Install and configure django-allauth with Google OAuth
description: Add django-allauth to project. Configure Google OAuth2 provider: create GCP OAuth credentials (client ID + secret), configure allauth settings (ACCOUNT_*, SOCIALACCOUNT_*), add allauth URLs. Verify Google login flow works end-to-end in staging.

title: Configure production environment on GAE
description: Set up production GAE version/service with its own Cloud SQL database (separate from staging). Configure manual deploy approval in CD pipeline for production. Set min_instances appropriately.

title: Set up staging environment on GAE
description: Create a staging GAE service (or version). Wire to staging Cloud SQL database. Ensure CD pipeline deploys here on merge to main. Add staging URL to ALLOWED_HOSTS.

title: Configure custom domain and HTTPS on GAE
description: Domain: wsde.rcel.biz (already owned). Verify domain ownership in Google Search Console / GCP, then map to GAE app. GAE provides managed SSL certs automatically. Update ALLOWED_HOSTS, CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS, and Wagtail Site records to use wsde.rcel.biz. Also configure www redirect if desired.

title: Write app.yaml and GAE service configuration
description: Write app.yaml for the default service. Configure: Python runtime, entrypoint (gunicorn or uvicorn), instance class, scaling settings (automatic, min_instances=0 for staging), env_variables (non-secret only), VPC connector for Cloud SQL private IP.

title: Configure Cloud Storage for static and media files
description: Create a GCS bucket for static files. Configure django-storages (or equivalent) for collectstatic → GCS. Set CORS and public read for static assets. Configure media file storage for any user uploads.

title: Provision Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance
description: Create a Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance for wsde. Configure: instance tier (start small, e.g. db-f1-micro for dev), private IP or Cloud SQL Auth Proxy, automated backups, create wsde database and user.

title: Configure Secret Manager for application credentials
description: Store all secrets in GCP Secret Manager: DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY, social auth client IDs/secrets, email credentials. Wire into Django settings via a secrets loader (e.g. google-cloud-secret-manager). Never put secrets in app.yaml or env files committed to repo.

title: Set up GCP project and enable required APIs
description: Create a fresh GCP project for wsde. Suggested project ID: wsde-rcel-biz or similar. Enable APIs: App Engine, Cloud SQL Admin, Secret Manager, Cloud Storage, Cloud Build (if needed for deploy). Set up billing alerts. Note: owner is starting fresh — no existing GCP project to reuse.

title: Set up .githooks for pre-commit quality gates
description: Configure .githooks (or pre-commit) to run ruff + ty before commit. Mirror menus/.githooks approach. Add setup step to Makefile.

title: Write AGENTS.md and copilot-instructions.md
description: Write AGENTS.md and .github/copilot-instructions.md for the wsde project. Pull from menus project conventions and adapt for wsde. Must cover all sections below.  ## Architecture conventions  Source code under src/. Settings hierarchy: base.py -> dev.py -> production.py -> local.py (gitignored). Default: core.settings.production.  ### Services Layer (MANDATORY for every app)  Each app must have a services/ directory: - services/data.py — Data-access layer. Public API for reading/writing the app's models. Other apps import this, never models directly. Large apps may split into services/data/ package with focused submodules; data/__init__.py re-exports via __all__. - services/logic.py — Business-logic layer. Views delegate here. Logic calls data.py for persistence.  Critical rule — mutation must go through the logic layer: - Views may call data.py for reads. - Views must never call data.py functions that mutate state. All mutations go through logic.py.  ### Models Minimise logic. Fields, Meta, __str__, simple computed properties only. No business logic.  ### Views HTTP concerns only: request parsing, auth checks, call services layer, return response. No business logic.  ## Testing (MANDATORY red/green TDD)  1. RED — Write failing test first. Run tests, confirm failure. 2. GREEN — Write minimal code to pass. Run tests, confirm green. 3. REFACTOR — Clean up. Re-run tests.  Rules: - Never write production code without a failing test. - Run tests after every RED and GREEN step. - After all tests pass, run make check and make fix. - Never leave failing tests.  ### Unit tests - Every function in services/data.py and services/logic.py must have tests. - Test happy paths and edge cases. - Use factory-boy factories (in each app's factories.py). - Use pytest.mark.django_db for database tests. - Test files: <app>/tests/test_data.py, <app>/tests/test_logic.py, <app>/tests/test_views.py. - Shared per-app fixtures in <app>/tests/conftest.py. Cross-app fixtures in src/conftest.py.  ### E2E tests (Playwright) - Every significant front-end feature must have Playwright e2e tests in e2e/. - Use make e2e (Docker, headless). - All navigation helpers live in e2e/helpers.py — never write local go_to_* helpers in test files.  #### e2e/helpers.py canonical helpers go(page, base_url, path)          — replaces page.goto + wait_for_load_state settle(page)                      — wait for networkidle after click/submit wait_for_htmx(page)               — signals HTMX was the trigger, waits for networkidle wait_for_htmx_target(page, sel)   — wait for HTMX to inject content into specific element wait_for_alpine(page, ms=300)     — short wait for Alpine reactive DOM updates wait_for_animation(page, ms=200)  — wait for CSS transitions to complete require_or_skip(value, reason)    — replaces if not x: return, reports as skipped pk_from_href(href)                — extract integer PK from a URL path string  Canonical test pattern:     from helpers import go, require_or_skip      class TestMyFeature:         def test_something(self, authenticated_page: Page, base_url: str) -> None:             go(authenticated_page, base_url, '/members/')             require_or_skip(authenticated_page.locator('.member-card').count(), 'no members')             # ... interact and assert  Never use page.goto(f'{base_url}/path/') — always go(page, base_url, '/path/'). Never use if not x: return — always require_or_skip(x, 'reason').  #### E2E gotchas (Alpine.js + Playwright + Docker) - CDN scripts don't work in Docker e2e — serve Alpine.js and HTMX from local static files, never CDN URLs. - Use x-show instead of x-if — x-if removes elements from DOM entirely; Playwright cannot find them before Alpine initialises. x-show keeps elements in DOM (hidden via display:none). Pair with x-cloak to prevent flash. - Wait for Alpine to render before interacting — after click() that triggers Alpine state change, add expect(element).to_be_visible() before pressing keys. -  in event handlers vs init() — inside @keydown on an input, this. is the input not the component root. Store this._root = this. in init() and use _root for DOM traversal. - Blur races with keyboard/cancel — x-show triggers blur (element stays in DOM). Enter/Escape + @blur='save()' causes double-fire. Use a _skipBlur flag in keyboard handlers. For cancel buttons use @mousedown.prevent instead of @click. - Locator specificity with x-show — both display and edit elements are always in DOM. Scope locators to visible elements.  ## Feature completeness checklist Every new model or feature must include: - Django admin with full ModelAdmin configuration - Complete services layer (CRUD in data.py, logic in logic.py) - Comprehensive tests via red/green TDD - E2E tests for user-facing features - factory-boy factory with sensible defaults - Sample data wired into data_sample management command  ## Frontend conventions Stack: Django/Wagtail templates + HTMX + Alpine.js + Tailwind CSS v4. No django-cotton. - Templates live inside each app at <app>/templates/. Base template in core/templates/. - URL patterns in core/urls.py — include new app URL confs from there. - Tailwind version pinned in Makefile (TW_VERSION). Binary gitignored, auto-downloaded. - BigAutoField for all primary keys. - Use uv for dependencies (not pip).

title: Configure GHA CD pipeline for GAE deploy
description: Write GitHub Actions deploy workflow: triggers on merge to main, deploys to GAE staging (and optionally production via manual approval). Uses OIDC or service account JSON secret for GCP auth.

title: Configure Dependabot for Python and GHA dependencies
description: Add .github/dependabot.yml to auto-raise PRs for outdated pip and GitHub Actions dependencies. Weekly schedule.

title: Configure pyproject.toml for ruff, ty, and pytest
description: Set up pyproject.toml with: ruff (lint + format, same rule set as menus), ty for type checking, pytest with django-pytest plugin, coverage config. Ensure `make check` and `make fix` work.

title: Write project Makefile
description: Write Makefile mirroring menus conventions: setup, up (if using Docker locally), check, fix, test, test-one, migrate, makemigrations, runserver, createsuperuser. Document targets in README.

title: Configure GHA CI pipeline
description: Write GitHub Actions workflow: lint (ruff check), type-check (ty), test (pytest). Runs on every PR and push to main. Use uv for dependency installation. Mirror menus .github/workflows/.

title: Set up uv-managed Django + Wagtail project skeleton
description: Initialise a Python project using uv. Install Django (latest stable) and Wagtail (latest stable). Follow src/ layout convention from menus project. Create core app with settings hierarchy: base.py → dev.py → production.py → local.py (gitignored).

title: Create GitHub repository with branch protection and CODEOWNERS
description: Initialise the GitHub repo for wsde. Configure: main branch protection (require PR, require CI pass), CODEOWNERS file, issue templates.

title: Spike: Apple Sign-In requirements
description:
Investigate what is required to implement Apple Sign-In. Key questions: (1) Apple requires an Apple Developer account and explicit domain/URL registration — what is needed for GAE-hosted apps? (2) Does django-allauth support Apple Sign-In out of the box? (3) What is the AASA (Apple App Site Association) story for web-only? (4) Are there any user-facing UX constraints (e.g. Apple hides email addresses).

## Context
Apple Sign-In is notoriously complex to set up vs Google. Time-box this to 2 hours. If it proves too painful, defer Apple support to a later milestone.

title: ADR: GAE Standard vs Flexible environment
description:
Decide whether to use App Engine Standard (managed runtimes, scales to zero, cheaper) or Flexible (Docker-based, more control, always-on minimum).

DECISION: Use GAE Standard Environment.

Rationale:
- Site is low-traffic for most of the year; Standard scales to zero, Flexible requires a minimum always-on instance.
- No exotic binary dependencies — Django, Wagtail, psycopg, Pillow all work fine in Standard's managed runtime.
- Python 3.12/3.13 are supported runtimes.
- Simpler ops: no Dockerfile to maintain for production, no image registry.
- Cloud SQL Auth Proxy is built into Standard via the cloud_sql_instances app.yaml setting — no VPC connector needed.
- Local dev will use Docker for environment parity (same pattern as the menus project).
- Free tier (28 F instance-hours/day) covers quiet periods.

Implementation notes:
- Use gunicorn or uvicorn as the WSGI/ASGI entrypoint in app.yaml.
- No persistent local filesystem — use Cloud SQL for data, GCS for static/media (already planned).
- Automatic managed SSL certs on custom domain (wsde.rcel.biz).

title: ADR: Frontend approach
description: Decide on the frontend stack for templates and interactivity.  DECISION: Wagtail/Django templates + HTMX + Alpine.js + Tailwind CSS v4. No django-cotton.  Rationale: - Same stack as the menus project — zero learning curve, solved problems reusable. - HTMX handles partial page updates (forms, dynamic lists, payment status) without SPA complexity. - Alpine.js handles UI state (dropdowns, toggles, modals) without a build step. - Tailwind CSS v4 via pinned CLI binary (same approach as menus Makefile). - django-cotton is explicitly excluded: HTML-escaping of x-data attributes breaks Alpine components; rendering edge cases cause issues that outweigh the DRY benefits. Use plain Django template includes and template tags instead. - No node_modules in production, no separate frontend build pipeline. - Playwright e2e tests work the same way as menus (same helpers.py patterns).  Implementation notes: - Serve Alpine.js and HTMX from local static files — never CDN URLs (Docker e2e cannot reach CDNs). - Use x-show instead of x-if — x-if removes elements from DOM, breaking Playwright locators. - Pin Tailwind version in Makefile (TW_VERSION), auto-download binary, gitignore it. - Templates live inside each app at <app>/templates/. Base template in core/templates/.

title: ADR: Social auth library choice
description:
Choose between django-allauth and python-social-auth (social-auth-app-django) for Google and Apple Sign-In.

DECISION: Use django-allauth.

Rationale:
- SocialAccountAdapter.is_open_for_signup() / pre_social_login() provides a clean, well-documented hook for the admin approval gate.
- Wagtail + allauth is a common, well-supported combination with community examples.
- Apple Sign-In is a first-class built-in provider.
- Includes local account management (email verification, welcome emails) at no extra cost.
- More actively maintained than python-social-auth; faster to pick up provider changes.

Implementation notes:
- Add django-allauth to dependencies via uv.
- Configure ACCOUNT_* and SOCIALACCOUNT_* settings in base.py.
- Override SocialAccountAdapter to enforce the approved-email gate.
- Apple Sign-In fiddliness (Services ID, private key, domain verification) is a separate concern covered by the spike (wsde-x9d.2), not a reason to change library.

title: Epic: Photo Albums
description: Link out to external photo albums (Google Photos, iCloud). Members-only album list with name, event year, and external URL.

title: Epic: Member Portal
description: Members-only authenticated area. Full event details, itinerary, member list, and member dashboard.

title: Epic: Financial Tracking
description: Track pre-payment pledges and kitty expenses. Admin records payments; members see their own status and a shared kitty summary.

title: Epic: Infrastructure & Deployment
description: GCP project setup, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL, Secret Manager, App Engine configuration, staging + production environments, static file serving via Cloud Storage, custom domain and HTTPS.

title: Epic: CMS & Public Site
description: Wagtail CMS installation and configuration. Public-facing promotional pages: homepage and event teaser visible without login.

title: Epic: Authentication & User Management
description: Social login via Google and Apple (django-allauth). Admin-approval gate so only permitted users can create accounts. User profile model.

title: Epic: Project Foundation
description: GitHub repo, project skeleton, CI/CD pipelines, code quality tooling (ruff, ty, uv, pytest), Makefile, and developer experience baseline. Mirrors menus project conventions.

title: M4: Members Portal Live
description: Authenticated members can log in and access full event details, the member list, and itinerary.

title: M5: Financials Tracking Live
description: Pre-payment pledges and kitty expenses can be tracked. Members can see their own payment status.

title: M3: Public Site Live
description: Minimal public-facing promotional site is live on production. Non-authenticated visitors see the landing page and event teaser.

title: M2: Auth Working
description: Members can log in via Google (and Apple). Non-members hit an approval gate. Admins can approve/reject requests.

title: M1: Skeleton Up
description: Repo exists, CI passes, hello-world Django+Wagtail app deploys to GAE staging. The foundation everything else builds on.

value: Git workflow: always create a feature branch from latest main (git pull --ff-only origin main) then push to that branch and open a PR. Never push directly to main. Work is not complete until a PR is open.
